title: Data loader hook for sessions and session items
purpose: Provides async functions to load sessions and session items from the API and populate the session store, with loading and error tracking.

steps.content: Initialize loading and error state variables.
Extract store setter and getter functions from the session store.
Return an object containing loading state, error state, and all data loading functions.
Set the loading state to true and clear any previous error.
Fetch all available sessions from the API.
Store the fetched sessions in the session store.
IF the API call fails, capture the error message and set it in state.
Reset the loading state to false after the operation completes.
Fetch the collaborative session state from the API.
Store the fetched collaborative state in the session store.
IF the API call fails, log the error and set the collaborative state to null.
Set the loading state to true and clear any previous error.
Fetch diagrams, documents, designs, spreadsheets, snippets, embeds, and images for the session in parallel.
Store the fetched items in their respective sections of the session store.
Fetch and store the collaborative session state.
IF any of the API calls fail, capture the error message and set it in state.
Reset the loading state to false after the operation completes.
Capture the current selection state from the session store.
Load fresh session items for the specified project and session.
Restore the previous selection if the selected item still exists in the refreshed data.
IF the selected item no longer exists, do not change the selection.

methods.name: selectDiagramWithContent
methods.normalized_params: (project: string, session: string, id: string): Promise<void>

steps.content: Set the selected diagram ID in the session store for immediate UI feedback.
Fetch the full content of the selected diagram from the API.
Update the diagram in the store with its fetched content.
IF the API call fails, capture the error message and set it in state.

methods.name: selectDocumentWithContent
methods.normalized_params: (project: string, session: string, id: string): Promise<void>

steps.content: Set the selected document ID in the session store for immediate UI feedback.

IF the API call fails, capture the error message and set it in state.
